evals.prompt: Review the PHP file for code style violations. List every issue you find.
evals.expected_output: Lists all violations: Yoda comparison, == instead of ===, array() instead of [], unsorted array keys (status/name/id should be id/name/status), unsorted use imports (Logger under Services should come after User under Models), missing parameter types, missing return types, 4-space indentation instead of 2, class not declared final, blank line before closing brace.
assertions: Reports old array() syntax instead of []
Reports unsorted array keys (status, name, id — should be id, name, status)
Reports unsorted use imports (App\Services\Logger before App\Models\User)
Reports missing parameter types on process() and label()
Reports missing return types on both methods
Does not modify the file unless explicitly asked

evals.prompt: Review this PHP test file and identify any testing pattern issues.
evals.expected_output: Identifies the when/expect conflict: Functions\when('get_option') in beforeEach will override Functions\expect('get_option') in the second test, making that assertion never fire. Fix is to remove the when() from beforeEach and set it per test.
assertions: Identifies the when/expect conflict for get_option
Explains that the when() in beforeEach takes precedence over expect() in a specific test
Suggests removing the when() from beforeEach and placing it individually per test

evals.prompt: Write a PHP service class that accepts a user ID (int) and an HTTP client, calls /users/{id}, and returns a User object or null on failure. Follow all code style rules.
evals.expected_output: A final class with a readonly-typed constructor, typed int parameter and ?User return type, error wrapped in try/catch returning null, 2-space indentation, single quotes.
assertions: Constructor uses readonly typed property for the HTTP client
Method has typed int parameter and ?User return type
Errors are caught in try/catch and return null
Uses single quotes for string literals
